articles.title: Oil edges lower, but heads for weekly gain as Middle East supply risks persist
articles.description: Oil prices saw a slight dip Friday but are set for weekly gains. US and Iran exchanged strikes, escalating regional tensions and impacting supply routes. Concerns over rising inflation are also pressuring oil demand and market prices. The conflict has delayed the reopening of the vital Strait of Hormuz waterway. Market sentiment remains cautious as leaders express hopes for de-escalation.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel prices today - 10 July: Check fuel rates in Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Kolkata as oil prices ease
articles.description: Petrol and diesel prices today: Fuel prices in India stood steady on Friday despite easing of Brent crude price. Check fuel rates in Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Kolkata and other cities

articles.title: Australian fuel giant suggests it may eventually phase out petrol
articles.description: Selling petrol is becoming less profitable in Australia, says Ampol's CEO, but there's money to be made in electric vehicle charging.

articles.title: Amid Ethanol Blending Row, Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri Clears Air on Claims And Misconceptions | Exclusive
articles.description: Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri addressed misconceptions regarding the blending of Ethanol (E20) with petrol in a recent interview., India News, Times Now

articles.title: Stock Market Live: Sensex jumps 830 pts, Nifty reclaims 24,200; VIX down 6%, all sectors gain
articles.description: Sensex Today | Stock Market LIVE Updates: The Indian benchmark indices opened higher on July 10 with Nifty above 24150, led by gains in IT stocks, while softer crude oil prices and positive global cues further boosted investor sentiment. TCS, Infosys, Shriram Finance, Hindalco, Adani Enterprises were among major gainers on the Nifty, while losers were Dr Reddy's Labs, Bharti Airtel, Apollo Hospitals. Except pharma, healthcare, all other sectoral indices are trading in the green with bank, IT, metal, Consumer Durables up 1% each. Nifty Midcap and smallcap indices up nearly 1% each.

articles.title: Global Market Today: Asian stocks rise following chip rally, oil slips
articles.description: Asian stocks climbed as investors favored semiconductor shares due to AI demand. Oil prices declined as energy supply disruption fears eased. South Korea's Kospi index saw a significant rally, while other regional benchmarks edged higher. Futures for the Nasdaq 100 Index indicated a more cautious market sentiment. Company earnings and profit growth will guide market direction next month.

articles.title: Asian shares gain after chip rally, oil prices slip
